author: developeruche
content: One average block (15 - 20mb witness size), it takes about 60ms. Yeah, this is round trip time from CL PoV

author: kevaundray
content: For the worse case, I guess we should clarify and say it’s worse case size?

author: developeruche
content: Yeah, would also include the average case

author: kevaundray
content: Yep was mostly thinking that it’s not the worse case block which has the max number of trie changes for example

author: kevaundray
content: It was mostly a worse case execution witness size (so it’s possible that a block with a ton of writes goes above 1 second for example is what I’m saying)

author: developeruche
content:
Yeah, I get what you are saying and I think that could be very correct... 

But just to be sure I would modify the spamming tool to simulate this, it's abit tricky though...

I think if I can spam a block whose txs have alot of write op would do justice to this.

My only concern is that, during testing, I am working with a node having a fresh trie, do you think i should be worried about that?

author: kevaundray
content: Ah right, I think so because it may not be representative of mainnet given the node depths will be shallow

author: kevaundray
content: We could leave it as an open question and measure it once we hook everything up to the EEST tests
